title_zh: olmo-eval：模型开发循环的评估工作平台
title_en: olmo-eval: An evaluation workbench for the model development loop
summary_zh: Hugging Face 博客介绍了 olmo-eval，这是一款专为模型开发循环设计的评估工作平台，旨在帮助研究人员和开发者更高效地评估和改进机器学习模型。
summary_en: The Hugging Face blog introduces olmo-eval, an evaluation workbench designed for the model development loop, aimed at helping researchers and developers efficiently evaluate and improve machine learning models.
why_it_matters_zh: olmo-eval 提供了一个系统化的工具，简化了模型评估过程，助力模型不断优化，推动人工智能研究与应用的发展。
why_it_matters_en: olmo-eval offers a systematic tool that simplifies the model evaluation process, facilitating continuous model improvements and advancing AI research and applications.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: olmo-eval: An evaluation workbench for the model development loop. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: ToolSense：用于审计大规模语言模型参数化工具知识的诊断框架
title_en: ToolSense: A Diagnostic Framework for Auditing Parametric Tool Knowledge in LLMs
summary_zh: 大型语言模型作为代理在庞大工具库中面临关键的工具检索瓶颈。为解决嵌入式检索方法对专业工具语义捕捉不足的问题，参数化工具检索通过将工具编码为虚拟标记并进行两阶段微调，实现了强劲的检索性能。现有基准测试在查询和解码上存在限制，难以评估模型是否真正理解工具。ToolSense是一个开源的基于LLM的诊断框架，能自动生成多级歧义现实检索基准、多项选择和问答探测基准。应用ToolSense至ToolBench及多种训练配置揭示知识与检索能力的脱节现象，部分模型尽管检索表现优异，但在事实探测上表现接近随机。该框架及基准已开源。
summary_en: Large language models acting as agents over extensive tool catalogs face a critical bottleneck in tool retrieval. To overcome embedding-based retrieval limitations in capturing specialized tool semantics, parametric tool retrieval encodes each tool as a virtual token appended to the vocabulary, fine-tuned in memorization and retrieval stages, achieving strong benchmark results. Existing benchmarks use verbose queries and constrained decoding, limiting insight into true tool understanding. ToolSense is an open-source LLM-powered diagnostic framework that automatically generates realistic retrieval benchmarks with varying ambiguity, multiple-choice, and QA probing benchmarks. Applying ToolSense to ToolBench and various training configurations reveals a dissociation between knowledge and retrieval: some models perform well on retrieval but poorly on factual probes. The framework and benchmarks are publicly available.
why_it_matters_zh: ToolSense框架弥补了现有工具检索评估的不足，通过多维度诊断测试揭示模型在检索性能与知识理解间可能存在的脱节，推动对参数化工具知识更深入的理解与改进。
why_it_matters_en: The ToolSense framework addresses limitations in current tool retrieval evaluations by introducing multi-faceted diagnostic tests that expose potential gaps between retrieval performance and genuine knowledge understanding, advancing deeper insights and improvements in parametric tool knowledge within LLMs.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: ToolSense: A Diagnostic Framework for Auditing Parametric Tool Knowledge in LLMs. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: Arbor：作为自主代理认知层的树搜索框架
title_en: Arbor: Tree Search as a Cognition Layer for Autonomous Agents
summary_zh: Arbor 是一个多智能体框架，利用结构化树搜索作为自主代理在大规模、有状态动作空间中的认知层。不同于以往的无状态孤立目标优化系统，Arbor 维护一个含评分假设的搜索树，作为智能体间的共享工作内存，随着每次测量不断进化，将失败视为重新探索的诊断信号。验证中，Arbor 在全栈大语言模型推理优化中实现多达193%的吞吐-延迟帕累托改进，远超单一智能体的33%提升且避免崩溃。系统通过协同的 Orchestrator 和 Critic 智能体分工保障稳定性，展现硬件无关性和高可复现性。
summary_en: Arbor is a multi-agent framework introducing structured tree search as a cognition layer for autonomous agents in large, stateful action spaces. Unlike prior stateless isolated optimizations, Arbor maintains a scored hypothesis search tree as shared working memory evolving with each measurement; failures guide exploration and successes shift bottlenecks. Validated on full-stack large language model inference optimization, Arbor achieves up to 193% throughput-latency Pareto improvements over vendor baselines, outperforming single agents capped at 33% gains who crash quickly. It uses coordinated Orchestrator and Critic agents for stability and generalizes across hardware generations with reproducible results.
why_it_matters_zh: Arbor 通过引入共享的结构化树搜索实现了智能体之间的有效协作与认知，解决了传统优化中无状态和孤立的问题。其架构实现了硬技能与软技能的分离，支持完全自主的长期优化任务。在全栈推理领域的成功验证展示了其对复杂、多层次系统优化的强大能力和稳定性，突破了性能瓶颈，体现了面向未来硬件的适应性和可靠性。
why_it_matters_en: Arbor leverages shared structured tree search to enable effective multi-agent cognition and collaboration, overcoming challenges of stateless and isolated optimizations. Its architecture decomposes agent capabilities into hard and soft skills, enabling fully autonomous long-term optimization. Successful validation in full-stack inference showcases its powerful and stable handling of complex systems, breaking performance bottlenecks and demonstrating adaptability and reliability across evolving hardware platforms.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Arbor: Tree Search as a Cognition Layer for Autonomous Agents. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 面向AI代理的战略决策支持
title_en: Strategic Decision Support for AI Agents
summary_zh: 传统上，决策支持研究人类如何利用机器学习模型做出更好的决策。然而，在现代代理系统中，角色逐渐颠倒：AI代理代表用户行动，而人类和工具成为支持机制。这种转变带来了可靠性问题，因为代理的错误可能有严重后果，且代理行为必须与人类目标和约束保持一致。本文提出了一个战略决策支持框架，通过优化问题最小化支持使用，同时控制代理孤立行动时因缺乏支持导致的关键错误概率。理论和在线算法验证了该方法能有效控制错误率，减少不必要的支持调用，并应用于信息收集、人机协作和工具使用等多场景，表现出良好效果。
summary_en: Traditionally, decision support focuses on how humans use machine learning models to improve decisions. In modern agentic systems, this role reverses: AI agents act on behalf of users, with humans and tools providing support. This reversal raises reliability concerns, as agent errors can be critical and must align with human goals. The authors propose a strategic decision support framework framing this as an optimization problem that minimizes support use while controlling the probability that agents act alone when support would have improved outcomes. They develop online algorithms that adaptively manage support requests, ensuring controlled error rates without distributional assumptions. This framework is applied across scenarios like information gathering, human-AI collaboration, and tool use, demonstrating effective error control and reduced support usage.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究从传统人机决策支持模式转向以AI代理为核心，回应了现代代理系统中角色逆转带来的新挑战。提出的理论框架和在线算法有效平衡了支持资源使用和决策质量，适用多种实际场景，对提升AI代理系统的可靠性和效率具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: This research shifts the traditional human-centered decision support paradigm to one centered on AI agents, addressing challenges arising from reversed roles in modern agentic systems. The proposed theoretical framework and online algorithms effectively balance support resource use and decision quality, applicable across various practical scenarios, significantly enhancing reliability and efficiency of AI agent systems.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Strategic Decision Support for AI Agents. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 帮助图表讲述故事！基于论文的复杂科学图视频生成
title_en: Helping Figures Tell their Story! Paper-Grounded Video Generation Explaining Complex Scientific Figures
summary_zh: 科学图表将复杂流程浓缩于一幅画布，但理解它们需基于论文的逐步讲解和视觉重点，这在现有视频生成系统和评测中缺失。为此，我们提出基于论文的图表转视频生成：从图表及其论文生成带解说且区域定位的演示视频。我们设计了MINARD（通过区域分解的多模态架构解读）管线，生成与论文忠实对应的解说并依序定位至图表区域。我们还发布了FigTalk基准，包含新的连续和组件级定位指标。在FigTalk上，MINARD生成类人且论文忠实的解说，并在人机评测及自动评测中在解说条件下图表空间定位方面超越现有方法。
summary_en: Scientific figures condense complex pipelines into a single canvas, but understanding them requires paper-grounded, step-by-step narration aligned with visual highlights—a capability missing from current video generation systems and benchmarks. To address this, we introduce paper-grounded figure-to-video generation, producing narrated and region-grounded walkthrough videos from a figure and its paper. We propose MINARD, a pipeline that generates paper-faithful narrations and grounds them sequentially to figure regions. We also release FigTalk, a benchmark with new sequential and component-level grounding metrics. On FigTalk, MINARD produces humanlike, paper-faithful narrations and outperforms existing approaches in narration-conditioned figure spatial grounding based on both automatic and human evaluations.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究解决了当前视频生成系统无法结合论文内容逐步讲述复杂科学图表的难题，提升了图表的可理解性和信息传播效率，对科学传播和教育具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: This work addresses the limitation of current video generation systems in providing paper-aligned, step-by-step narration of complex scientific figures, enhancing figure comprehensibility and information dissemination, which is important for scientific communication and education.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Helping Figures Tell their Story! Paper-Grounded Video Generation Explaining Complex Scientific Figures. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: MARD：机制级药物相互作用预测的镜像增强推理蒸馏方法
title_en: MARD: Mirror-Augmented Reasoning Distillation for Mechanism-Level Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种机制层面的药物相互作用（DDI）预测方法，能够识别涉及具体酶或药效学轴线、作用方向及证据，而不仅是判断药物间是否存在相互作用。作者设计了一个包含7大类147子类型的结构化DDI标注及评估协议，并提出了结合三种训练创新的7B参数规模镜像增强推理蒸馏（MARD）模型。该模型在2026年4月DrugBank数据上表现优异，在32个系统中，唯一保持药物对新颖性下的高准确率，超过最佳基线13.9个百分点，优于GPT-4o 6.7个百分点，且推理基于结构化药理知识而非药物频率记忆，成本仅为顶尖API的1%。同时，相关语料库和代码已公开。
summary_en: This paper introduces a mechanism-level drug-drug interaction (DDI) prediction method that identifies the specific enzyme or pharmacodynamic axis involved, the interaction direction, and supporting evidence, beyond simple interaction classification. A structured 7-family/147-subtype taxonomy and leakage-safe evaluation protocol are proposed. The authors develop a 7-billion parameter Mirror-Augmented Reasoning Distillation (MARD) model combining three training innovations. Tested on the April 2026 DrugBank release, MARD outperforms 32 other systems, maintaining accuracy on novel drug pairs, surpassing the best baseline by 13.9 percentage points and GPT-4o by 6.7 points with about 1% of the API cost. The improvements arise from structured pharmacological reasoning rather than memorization. The dataset, retrieval index, and training code are publicly released.
why_it_matters_zh: 机制级DDI预测对于安全合理用药至关重要，现有方法多停留在粗粒度交互判定，缺乏对具体作用机制的识别和解释。MARD通过创新的蒸馏训练与严谨评估协议提升了对未知药物对的泛化能力和推理透明度，降低了成本并促进相关研究复现和应用推广，具备重要的科学和实际价值。
why_it_matters_en: Mechanism-level DDI prediction is critical for ensuring drug safety and rational use, but existing methods mostly deliver coarse interaction judgments without detailed mechanism insight or interpretability. MARD enhances generalization to novel drug pairs and reasoning transparency through innovative distillation training and rigorous evaluation protocols, reducing costs while facilitating reproducibility and real-world application. This represents significant scientific and practical advancement.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: MARD: Mirror-Augmented Reasoning Distillation for Mechanism-Level Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 通过波斯谚语条件故事生成实现大型语言模型中的语义受限解压
title_en: Constrained Semantic Decompression in LLMs through Persian Proverb-Conditioned Story Generation
summary_zh: 该研究将将抽象且富有内涵的谚语转化为生动且忠实于其道德寓意的故事，定义为一种“受限语义解压”任务。以波斯语谚语为例，团队构建了谚语对齐叙事数据集（PAND），结合人类撰写的故事和明确的寓意。通过人类校准的LLM评审和结构化指标的混合评估，分析了模型在不同提示条件下的表现。结果显示，目前的LLM虽然在语言流畅度上表现良好，但常常无法准确体现谚语中蕴含的道德和因果结构。研究还发现，借助显式推理和迭代优化可以部分缓解该问题，表明解压错误主要源于将抽象意义转化为叙事形式的难度，而非知识缺乏。该任务可扩展至其他压缩文化知识的形式。
summary_en: This study frames the task of transforming dense, abstract proverbs into engaging and morally faithful narratives as constrained semantic decompression. Focusing on Persian proverbs, the authors introduce the Proverb Aligned Narrative Dataset (PAND) that pairs proverbs with human-written stories and explicit meanings. Using a hybrid evaluation combining human-calibrated LLM judges and structural metrics, they analyze model behaviors under various prompting strategies. Findings show a consistent decompression gap: current large language models often produce fluent stories but fail to accurately realize the underlying moral and causal structures of the proverbs. Explicit reasoning and iterative refinement partially mitigate these failures, suggesting difficulties lie in translating abstract meaning into narrative rather than lack of knowledge. The task framework is extendable to other forms of compressed cultural knowledge.
why_it_matters_zh: 本研究揭示了目前大型语言模型在理解和生成文化深层含义文本时的局限，特别是在将抽象谚语语义准确展开为故事方面的差距。通过构建数据集和创新评估方法，展示了显式推理和反复改进的潜力，为未来提升模型对复杂文化知识的掌握提供方向。
why_it_matters_en: This research highlights the limitations of current large language models in faithfully expanding abstract cultural meanings into narratives, specifically the gap in semantic decompression of proverbs. By constructing a specialized dataset and innovative evaluation framework, the study demonstrates the potential of explicit reasoning and iterative refinement to improve model performance, guiding future advances in handling complex cultural knowledge.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Constrained Semantic Decompression in LLMs through Persian Proverb-Conditioned Story Generation. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_en: Profiling in PyTorch (Part 2): From nn.Linear to a Fused MLP
summary_zh: 本文介绍了如何使用PyTorch进行性能分析，重点展示了从简单的线性层（nn.Linear）到融合的多层感知机（MLP）的优化过程。
summary_en: This article demonstrates how to profile performance in PyTorch, focusing on the optimization process from a simple linear layer (nn.Linear) to a fused multilayer perceptron (MLP).
why_it_matters_zh: 了解如何分析和优化PyTorch模型性能，有助于提升深度学习模型的运行效率。
